changes: Every sidebar row now ends in two buttons: delete, plus the verb that row owns — + to add a worktree or session, ⋯ on a session. Bigger targets, and delete still asks first.
The liveness sphere is lit evenly from its core out to the rim, so it reads as a ball rather than a flat cluster of dots.

Browser tools keep working on a busy engine. A browser holding dozens of tabs used to fail every call with an opaque timeout; there are also new health and cookie tools, and snapshots can be scoped to a selector.
The working indicator is now a violet sphere that pulses outward in rings. Violet keeps it clearly apart from the blue needs-input mark, which the previous cyan lattice could resemble at a glance.
Synth now downloads as a disk image you drag into Applications. Running it straight from Downloads could leave it unable to find its own tooling, and on some Macs macOS refused to open it at all.
A working session now shows a cyan lattice that beats outward from its centre, replacing the amber dot — easier to tell apart from the blue unread mark, and it reads as alive at a glance.
Fixed a crash that could close Synth the moment a web page reached for Bluetooth.
Crashes are captured again instead of silently vanishing, so the ones that slip through get reported and fixed.
Toasts now anchor to the shell's corner and drain a countdown bar as they auto-dismiss — hover, or switching away from Synth, pauses the clock; input and error toasts stay until you act.
The active pane is marked with a top-edge bar in the mark's colour, and clicking anywhere in a pane focuses it.
A background branch keeps its split layout — switch away and back and the panes are still there.
Reorder drags mark the drop slot with a copper line instead of reshuffling the list under your cursor (sidebar and Settings templates).
Splits stay within one branch — a cross-branch drop now refuses instead of silently dropping the pane.
The in-app Changelog is a keyboard-driven two-pane sheet.
Split the content area into panes — drag a session onto an edge, or split by keyboard (⌘⇧+arrows); each branch remembers its own layout, ⌘⇧⏎ zooms a pane full-screen, and ⌘⇧U unsplits.
Browser page zoom (⌘+ / ⌘−), with a click-to-reset zoom badge in the address bar.
One command menu — the ⋯ kebab and right-click now match ⌘K, and negative actions (Close, Remove, Delete, Detach) are red.
Agents launch reliably even when your shell stops to ask about its own updates.
New worktrees fork off the default branch and start with no sessions.
Read what changed from the new in-app Changelog, and a fuller ⌘? shortcuts sheet.
Anonymous, opt-out product usage analytics — no login, no content on the wire, off on dev builds; turn it off in Settings → Privacy.
Native crash reporter so a crash is reported on the next launch.
Every quit now confirms with a single dialog, naming any busy sessions the quit would end.
Reissue of 0.4.0 — no functional changes.
Browser device mode (⌘⇧M) — render the page in a real device frame at true device viewports; agents can drive it too.
Pull-request indicators — each branch's PR state in the sidebar, and a clickable #number chip in the session header.
Copy the branch name from the pane header with one click.
Notifications now post from the open session, and the sidebar gets a restyle.
⌘D closes the current session — the focused sidebar row, or the open session — and Close always confirms first.
synth-app MCP server — agents can create worktrees with your approval and hand off to a fresh session; off by default, opt in from Settings.
Sidebar drops its indent guide lines — indentation alone carries the hierarchy.
⌘N opens the new-session picker for the branch you're in.
Browser sessions record video (browser_record_start / browser_record_stop).
Background self-updates, and a renderer that survives display changes.
First public release of Synth — an AI-first, Mac-native dev environment.
Sidebar of projects → branches → sessions with keyboard-first navigation and dark mode.
Embedded Ghostty terminals, Claude Code and OpenCode agents, and in-pane browser sessions.
Command palette (⌘K) for navigating and acting on anything, and a ⌘? shortcuts sheet.
Signed, notarized, self-updating builds.
